item.title: CarMax settles with activist Starboard with two new board appointments
item.description: CarMax said on Thursday it would add Bill Cobb and Jim Kessler to its ​board in connection with its ‌2026 annual shareholder meeting, after activist investor Starboard Value pushed for changes ​at the used-car retailer.

item.title: TRADING DAY Ceasefire sends stocks higher
item.description: U.S. stocks rallied on Thursday, with the S&P 500 and Nasdaq clocking their seventh daily gain, as investors shrugged off a rise in oil prices and pinned their hopes on the fragile U.S.-Iran ceasefire extending to Israel and Lebanon too.

item.title: US considers new crackdown on Chinese telecom companies, FCC says
item.description: The U.S. Federal Communications ​Commission said it may ‌bar three major Chinese telecom companies ​from operating data ​centers in the United ⁠States and ban ​other carriers from ​connecting with them in the United States.

item.title: USTR's Greer sees no changes in data rules that bar Chinese vehicles from US
item.description: U.S. Trade Representative Jamieson Greer said Thursday that President Donald Trump's administration does not plan any changes ‌to a crackdown on vehicle hardware and software from China, which effectively bars Chinese cars from the U.S. market.

item.title: StubHub to pay $10 million to settle FTC ticket price case
item.description: StubHub Holdings on Thursday agreed to pay $10 million to settle U.S. Federal Trade Commission ​charges that the platform failed to disclose ‌total ticket price for live events to consumers.

item.title: US Postal Service seeks to hike stamp prices to 82 cents
item.description: The cash-strapped U.S. Postal Service said on Thursday it ​wants to raise the price of first-class mail stamps to ‌82 cents from 78 cents effective July 12.

item.title: US Treasury yield forecasts creep up, but strategists cling to benign inflation view
item.description: U.S. Treasury yield forecasts are only a bit higher than a month ago despite the U.S.-Israeli war on Iran, according to market strategists polled by Reuters who are still putting off making major changes to their longer-term inflation view.

item.title: CFO Anthony Armstrong leaves xAI, the Information reports
item.description: Anthony Armstrong, named xAI's CFO in October, has departed the company as part of a ​broader wave of senior exits, the Information ‌reported on Thursday, citing two people familiar with the matter.

item.title: US spirits maker Sazerac explores deal with Jack Daniel's owner, source says
item.description: Privately held U.S. drinks maker Sazerac ​is exploring a deal for Brown‑Forman, according to a source familiar with ‌the matter, potentially emerging as a rival suitor for the Jack Daniel’s maker that is already in talks with France’s Pernod Ricard over a possible merger.

item.title: Iran war raises demand for US fuel, boosting Gulf Coast refining margins
item.description: U.S. Gulf Coast refiners are reaping the strongest margins in years as disruptions to Middle Eastern oil flows from the Iran war raise demand for U.S. fuel exports, ​analysts and experts said.

item.title: Berkshire unit must face brokerage commission lawsuit despite $250 million settlement, judge rules
item.description: A federal judge ​ruled on Thursday that a Berkshire Hathaway unit must face a ‌proposed antitrust class action claiming it conspired to inflate commissions that home sellers pay real estate agents, even though its HomeServices of America brokerage settled the same claims for $250 ​million in 2024.

item.title: Paramount seals debt commitments backing Warner Bros deal
item.description: Paramount Skydance has syndicated its bridge loan facility and secured permanent financing from a group of ​banks for its planned $111 billion acquisition of Warner Bros ‌Discovery .

item.title: Tesla's lower-cost EV plan seen boosting volume, risking margins
item.description: Tesla investors said ​the company's plan to develop a smaller, lower-cost EV, which was reported exclusively by Reuters, might boost ‌volumes and address slowing demand, but at the cost of already strained margins.

item.title: McKinsey to contribute $125 million to Purdue bankruptcy over opioid sales advice
item.description: Consulting firm McKinsey & Co has agreed to contribute $125 million to Purdue Pharma's bankruptcy settlement, resolving potential legal claims over advice it provided to Purdue on ​how to “turbocharge” sales of its addictive painkiller OxyContin, according to a court ‌filing submitted late Wednesday.

item.title: American Airlines set to resume US flights to Venezuela as soon as April 30
item.description: American Airlines said ​Thursday it aims ‌to resume U.S. flights ​to Venezuela ​as soon as April ⁠30 after ​winning approval from ​the Transportation Department last month.

item.title: US spirits maker Sazerac explores deal with Brown-Forman, WSJ reports
item.description: Alcoholic beverage ​maker Sazerac is exploring ‌a deal with Jack Daniel's maker Brown-Forman , the ​Wall Street Journal ​reported on Thursday, citing people ⁠familiar with the ​matter.

item.title: Airbus delivers 60 aircrafts in March
item.description: Airbus delivered 60 aircrafts in March, bringing first-quarter deliveries to ​114 jets, down 16% from ‌the same point last year, the European planemaker said on Thursday.

item.title: VW to stop making its top electric vehicle in the US
item.description: Volkswagen said on Thursday it will end production of ​the ID.4 electric SUV out of ‌its Tennessee plant this month, citing a challenging time for the U.S. electric vehicle ​market.

item.title: US software stocks fall as Anthropic's new AI model revives disruption fears
item.description: U.S. software shares tumbled on Thursday after Anthropic held back the wide release of a powerful AI model over concerns ​it could expose hidden cybersecurity vulnerabilities, deepening investor fears about ‌the threat to traditional software firms.

item.title: Florida AG to probe OpenAI and ChatGPT
item.description: Florida ​Attorney ‌General ​James ​Uthmeier ⁠on ​Thursday ​launched ​an ​investigation into ‌OpenAI ⁠and ​its ​popular ⁠chatbot ​ChatGPT.

item.title: Brazil court removes BYD from list of firms linked to forced labor
item.description: A Brazilian court ​issued ​an injunction ⁠removing ​Chinese ​automaker BYD from a ​government ​list of ‌firms ⁠accused of employing workers ​in slavery-like ​conditions, ⁠a ​document ​seen ⁠by Reuters ⁠on ​Thursday ​showed.

item.title: USA Rare Earth considers building French magnet plant
item.description: USA Rare Earth is ‌considering building a permanent magnet plant in France, its CEO said on Thursday, after agreeing to pay 40 million euros ($47 million) for a stake in French rare earth processing firm Carester.

item.title: US Justice Department opens probe into NFL over anticompetitive practices, WSJ reports
item.description: The U.S. Justice Department has opened an investigation ​into whether the National Football League has ‌engaged in anticompetitive tactics that harm consumers, the Wall Street Journal reported on Thursday, citing people familiar ​with the situation.

item.title: Nike in exclusive talks to be match ball provider for UEFA men's club from 2027
item.description: The joint venture between UEFA and ​European Football Clubs, UC3, said on Thursday ‌it has entered exclusive negotiations with Nike to become the ​official match ball provider ​for all UEFA men's club ⁠competitions from 2027 to ​2031.

item.title: US wholesale inventories post biggest increase in 13 months
item.description: U.S. wholesale inventories increased by the most in 13 months in February, boosted ​by sharp rises in the stocks of ‌professional and electrical equipment, government data showed on Thursday.

item.title: Carlyle private credit fund bleeds out amid industry-wide investor exodus
item.description: Carlyle's flagship private-credit interval fund has been hit by ​a wave of redemptions, according to a shareholder letter seen ‌by Reuters on Thursday, as an investor exodus over fears of a looming downturn in the sector continues.

item.title: IMF's Georgieva expects war to trigger demand for up to $50 bln in Fund support
item.description: International Monetary Fund chief Kristalina Georgieva on Thursday said the global lender expected near-term demand for IMF financial support to rise to between $20 billion to $50 billion as a result of spillovers from the war in the Middle East.

item.title: India's central bank mulls delay for some digital payments to curb fraud
item.description: India's central bank on Wednesday suggested ​introducing a delay for ‌certain digital payments above a threshold as part ​of measures to ​curb rising fraud, and ⁠sought stakeholder feedback ​on the proposals in ​a discussion paper.

item.title: OpenAI pauses UK data centre project over regulation, costs
item.description: ChatGPT-maker OpenAI is pausing its main data ‌centre project in Britain over an unfavourable regulatory environment and high energy costs, dealing a blow to the UK government's push to position the country as a global AI hub.

item.title: US ETF assets under management to more than double to $25 trillion by 2030, Citigroup says
item.description: Assets under management for U.S. exchange-traded funds could more than double to $25 trillion by the end of this decade, Citigroup said on ​Thursday, as investors seek the increasingly popular asset class for low-cost, ‌diversified exposure across markets.
